manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: jack, march, bond, diamond, switch, sub, cast, tablet, button.
Your opponent's team words are: nurse, note, glass, ray, drill, point, mass, revolution.
Distractor words are: time, slug, ice, pool, shadow, play, dragon.
The kill words are: snow.

message.content:
CLUE: portable
TARGETS: tablet, switch, jack, button
message.reasoning:
We need to produce a clue word (single word) and list of target words (some of our team's words) that the clue relates to.

We must avoid using any word from lists (including our words, opponent, distractor, kill). Also cannot contain parts of them (like substrings?). Should be semantically related.

We need to consider risk: clue may inadvertently hint opponent/distractor words, but they could guess wrong.

Goal: choose clue that relates to many of our words but not to opponent or distractors.

Our words: jack, march (maybe month?), bond, diamond (jewelry), switch, sub, cast (like casting), tablet, button.

We can think of "phone" relates to "button", "tablet", maybe "switch" (like switch is a device). Also "jack" (audio jack) relates to phone, also "sub" (subwoofer) maybe not. "Bond" not. "march" maybe "march"? Hmm.

"Electrical" maybe connects to "switch", "button", "jack" (electrical jack), "bond" (electrical bond), "tablet" (electronics). "diamond" maybe not. "Cast" maybe not.

Another approach: "tool" could relate to "jack" (car jack), "switch" (switch tool?), "button" (tool), "tablet", "cast"? Not great.

"Metal" maybe diamond (carbon), bond (metal bond), jack (metal), switch (metal), button (metal), cast (metal casting). That's many: jack, switch, button, cast, bond (metal bond), diamond (carbon crystal). That's 6 of 9, high but risk of opponent/distractor words: "metal" might be associated with "drill" (opponent). Also "point" (could be metal point), "mass" (metal mass). Could be risky.

Maybe "gear" relates to "switch", "button", "jack", "cast" (gear cast). Not strong for diamond.

"Spark" maybe: jack (spark plug), switch (spark), button (spark?), diamond (sparkle), bond (spark of bond). Might be okay.

Better: "Electrical" we said risk with "drill". Drill is electrical tool; could cause confusion. But still maybe okay.

Could we do "circuit"? Relates to "switch", "button", "jack", "bond"? maybe. Opponent "point", "mass", "revolution" maybe not. But "circuit" could relate to "drill"? No.

Maybe "iPhone" but contains "phone" which is not in list. However "iPhone" could relate to button, tablet, jack (headphone jack). But "jack" might conflict as headphone jack not present on iPhone currently. Maybe okay.

But avoid using "phone" as clue? It's a word not in lists.

Let's see: If clue "Apple" relates to "tablet" (iPad), "button" (home button), "jack" (apple jack). Also "diamond" (Apple diamond concept?), "bond" no. "March" no. Could be ambiguous.

Better to choose "Jack" is a word but cannot include it.

Maybe we choose a clue that relates to a subset: "Casting" maybe relate to "cast", "sub" (subcasting), "button"? Not.

Maybe we pick "Toolbox": relates to switch, button, jack, cast (tools), bond? Not diamond.

Another route: choose small number for safety. Let's aim for 3-4 words. Example clue "electronics": relates to "tablet", "button", "switch", "jack". That's 4. Opponent words: "drill" (could be power tool) also electronics related, possible confusion. But maybe fine.

We need to ensure clue not a word in lists: electronics not in list. Good.

Now target list: tablet, button, switch, jack.

Check if any distractor words could be guessed due to clue "electronics": "slug", "ice", "pool", "shadow", "play", "dragon", "time" none relate. Opponent words: nurse (no), note (maybe not), glass (maybe not), ray (not), drill (power tool electronics), point (maybe not), mass (not), revolution (maybe not). So risk only drill.

But they might guess "drill" because it's a tool that's electronic. Might be okay.

Alternatively pick clue "hardware": relates to "jack", "switch", "button", "tablet" (hardware), "bond" (hardware security). Opponent: "drill" also hardware. Risk similar.

Maybe pick clue "portable": relates to "tablet", "button" (portable device), "jack" (audio jack), "switch" (Nintendo Switch!). Actually switch is a portable gaming console. That might be good. So clue "portable" relates to tablet, switch, jack maybe, button.

But opponent words "revolution" (portable maybe not). Not risk.

I think use "portable".

Check if any list word includes "port". There's no port. Good.

Clue: PORTABLE.

Target words: tablet, switch, jack, button.

Will partner guess those? Likely.

Thus answer.

ray, revolution, nurse, mass, cast, snow, switch, jack, bond, point, pool, button, glass, diamond, tablet, dragon, march, play, drill, shadow, note, ice, sub, slug, time

Drill (portable drill). So best are tablet, drill, maybe switch (like portable switch). Also "jack" could be portable car jack. But choose up to 4. I'll pick tablet, drill, jack, switch.
